sutta: the buddha said monks there are these 4 floods of merit floods of things profitable bringing happiness giving the best things whose fruit is happiness leading to the heaven world leading to what is dear delightful and pleasant to profit and happiness what are the 4 to him monks who is the giver of a rope using which a monk is able to attain and abide in unbounded concentration of mind to him comes a flood of merit a flood of things profitable bringing happiness to him monks who is the giver of arms food using which a monk is able to attain and abide in unbounded concentration of mind to him comes a flood of merit a flood of things profitable bringing happiness to him monks who is the giver of lodging using which among is able to attain and abide in unbounded concentration of mind to him comes a flood of merit a flood of things profitable bringing happiness to him monks who is the giver of requisites and medicines for use in sickness using which among is able to attain and abide in unbounded concentration of mind to him comes a flood of merit a flood of things profitable bringing happiness giving the best things whose fruit is happiness leading to the heaven world leading to what is dear and delightful and pleasant to profit and happiness these monks are the 4 floods of merit flood floods of things profitable moreover monks if an aryan disciple be possessed of these 4 floods of merit floods of things profitable it is no easy thing to take the measure of his merit so as to say such and such is the extent of the flood of merit and so forth no it is to be reckoned as an incalculable immeasurable mighty mass of merit just as monks it is no easy thing to take the measure of the water in the mighty ocean so as to say so many gallons of water so many hundreds so many thousands so many hundreds of thousands of gallons of water no it is to be reckoned as an incalculable immeasurable mighty mass of merit
commentary: so in this suta the buddha is saying if you give the requisites to a monk and the monk uses that the requisite so that he can practice and he attains unbounded concentration of mind and then you as a supporter will come will get a flood of merit now which is so great now that it is immeasurable how is it now the buddha is talking about among getting unbounded concentration of mind why doesn't the buddha say wisdom because unbounded concentration of mind leads to wisdom that is what is implied here the buddha is not saying that a person gets wisdom straight away because the precondition for wisdom is unbounded concentration deep concentration of mind that's why uh concentration is always stressed by the buddha elsewhere later on you see some other suta where the buddha said that the condition for wisdom is this deep concentration now we come to

items: the giver of requisites and medicines for use in sickness
